verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira Afternoon — 16:00
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

conversationalSummary.text: Almagreira at Afternoon — 16:00 looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the weak source agreement could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 42 kJ, 4 s period, lully 19/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 114 kJ, 11 s period, consistent 66/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at low tide is fast and shallow in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for evening — 17:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide helps a bit, but the bay still needs enough push.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
10-13s at low tide is fast and shallow in the sheltered bay
Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 42 kJ, 11 s period, lully 29/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 26 kJ, 10 s period, lully 17/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at low tide can be a bit shallow and uneven on the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
